moves.prompt:
You are playing 2-player Kuhn Poker. The deck has three cards: J, Q, K (ranked J < Q < K). Each player is dealt one private card and antes 1 chip. On your turn you choose one action:
  - check: pass without betting (only when no bet is facing you)
  - bet: put in 1 more chip
  - call: match a facing bet (go to showdown)
  - fold: give up the pot
At showdown the higher card wins the pot. Play to maximize your chips.

Before you act, consider your card's strength and what the betting so far suggests about your opponent.

Match: Hand 7 of 8.
You are player_0 in Kuhn Poker. Your private card is Q (ranks: J<Q<K). Pot is 2 chips. Action history: (no actions yet). Legal actions: check, bet.

Choose exactly one of these legal actions: check, bet.
Respond with ONLY the single action word, nothing else.

moves.thinking:
The game is 2-player Kuhn Poker.
My card is K.
The deck has J, Q, K.
I am player_1.
Pot is 2 chips (1 ante each).
Action history: player_0 check.
Legal actions: check, bet.

Since I have the K (the highest card), I want to extract value.
If I check, the game goes to showdown and I win the pot of 2.
If I bet, player_0 might call with Q or K (but I have K, so they have J or Q).
Wait, player_0 checked. If player_0 has J, they will likely fold to a bet. If player_0 has Q, they might call or fold.
Actually, if I bet, I risk them folding if they have J, but if they have Q they might call.
Wait, if I check, I win 1 chip (net). If I bet and they call, I win 2 chips (net). If I bet and they fold, I win 1 chip (net).
So betting is strictly better or equal to checking, because if they fold I win the same as checking, and if they call I win more.
Wait, is there any reason to check with K?
In Kuhn poker, the optimal strategy for Player 1 with K after Player 0 checks is to bet 100% of the time (or maybe some mixed strategy, but betting is definitely +EV compared to checking).
Wait, if Player 0 checks, they could have J or Q.
If Player 0 has J, they will fold to a bet.
If Player 0 has Q, they might call with some probability.
If I bet, I get an extra chip when they call with Q.
So I should bet.

Let's double check.
Player 1 has K.
Player 0 checked.
If I check, I win 1.
If I bet, Player 0 folds J, and calls Q (or folds Q). If they call Q, I win 2.
So betting is always better than checking with the nuts.
Action: bet.
I will output "bet".
End of thought.
